author: Clement of Rome
book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.

classification.cautionReason: Valued early Christian text, but not part of the biblical canon for mainstream Christian denominations.

lineage: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
It is the Part of the Wicked to Vex the Righteous.

ccel.title: ANF09. The Gospel of Peter, The Diatessaron of Tatian, The Apocalypse of Peter, the Vision of Paul, The Apocalypse of the Virgin and Sedrach, The Testament of Abraham, The Acts of Xanthippe and Polyxena, The Narrative of Zosimus, The Apology of Aristides, The Epistles of Clement (complete text), Origen’s Commentary on John, Books 1–10, and Commentary on Matthew, Books 1, 2, and 10–14.

title: It is the Part of the Wicked to Vex the Righteous.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Ye are fond of contention, brethren, and full of zeal about things which do not pertain to salvation.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Look carefully into the Scriptures, which are the true utterances of the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Observe that nothing of an unjust or counterfeit character is written in them.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: There you will not find that the righteous were cast off by men who themselves were holy.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: The righteous were indeed persecuted, but only by the wicked.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: They were cast into prison, but only by the unholy; they were stoned, but only by transgressors; they were slain, but only by the accursed, and such as had conceived an unrighteous envy against them.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Exposed to such sufferings, they endured them gloriously.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: For what shall we say, brethren?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Was Daniel cast into the den of lions by such as feared God?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Were Ananias, and Azarias, and Michael shut up in a furnace of fire by those who observed the great and glorious worship of the Most High?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Far from us be such a thought!

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: Who, then, were they that did such things?

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: The hateful, and those full of all wickedness, were roused to such a pitch of fury, that they inflicted torture on those who served God with a holy and blameless purpose [of heart], not knowing that the Most High is the Defender and Protector of all such as with a pure conscience venerate His all-excellent name; to whom be glory for ever and ever.

verses.book: The First Epistle of Clement to the Corinthians.
verses.text: But they who with confidence endured [these things] are now heirs of glory and honour, and have been exalted and made illustrious by God in their memorial for ever and ever.
